task.title: Setting the default gateway on an existing connection by using nmstatectl

li.p: At least one static IP address must be configured on the connection on which the default gateway will be set.
The enp1s0 interface is configured, and the IP address of the default gateway is within the subnet of the IP configuration of this interface.

context.p: You can use the declarative Nmstate API to set or update the default gateway in a connection. Nmstate ensures that the result matches the configuration file or rolls back the changes.

step.cmd: Create a YAML file, for example ~/set-default-gateway.yml, with the following content:

info.codeblock: --- routes: config: - destination: 0.0.0.0/0 next-hop-address: 192.0.2.1 next-hop-interface: enp1s0
info.p: These settings define 192.0.2.1 as the default gateway, and the default gateway is reachable through the enp1s0 interface.

step.cmd: Apply the settings to the system:
